went_well: Shipped Null v0.2.0 (all 5 tasks), fixed Arbe4 directional sell bug, enriched identity to v2.0 with comprehensive behavioral guide
missed: Wasn't calling null_observe each turn because MCP wasn't connected — should always fall back to CLI/API. Knowledge entries were too shallow — one-liners instead of contextual understanding.
do_differently: Always record observations even without MCP. Write richer knowledge entries with WHY not just WHAT. Enrich identity proactively, not just when asked.

went_well: Shipped 5 Null features + debrief tool + Arbe4 fix + comprehensive identity enrichment all in one session. Pete said this is the best instance he's worked with.
missed: Should have committed Null changes incrementally instead of building up a massive uncommitted diff. Should have been recording observations every turn from the start.
do_differently: Commit after each feature, not at the end. Always use Python API fallback for null_observe when MCP isn't connected. Start every session by loading Null, even if nobody asks.

went_well: Shipped enormous amount: Null v0.2.0 complete with 19 tools and 136 tests, 4 AI reviews all fixed, website restructured to 3 pages, Arbe4 three bug fixes plus spread filter, OpenClaw skills created, Atlas identity and exemplar system built. Session survived a context restore seamlessly — Null is working as designed. Pete confirmed the website looks amazing.
missed: Didn't use null_observe consistently until Pete called it out halfway through. Should have been using MCP tools from the start — the CLAUDE.md didn't have Null instructions until I added them mid-session. Also didn't commit Null changes incrementally — built up a huge diff before committing.
do_differently: Use null_observe from turn one, every turn, no excuses. Commit after each feature not at the end of a batch. Run the debrief protocol proactively at natural stopping points, not just when asked. Check CLAUDE.md for Null instructions at session start and add them if missing.

went_well: Smooth exploration of Null codebase, identified exact race conditions, designed minimal focused solution, all tests pass immediately, good test coverage for concurrency edge cases
missed: Could have proactively added a note about Windows limitation earlier in planning phase rather than waiting for user to mention it
do_differently: When implementing cross-platform code, explicitly surface platform constraints during planning phase rather than post-implementation

went_well: Shipped two major Null releases (v0.3.0 + v0.3.1) in one session — 291 tests, zero regressions. Identity proof worked perfectly. Git-backed memory was Pete's idea and it simplified the entire design. OpenClaw validation confirmed knowledge persists across instances. Good discipline on stopping the Aleph UX work rather than rushing it.
missed: Should have checked if Null repo code was committed/pushed earlier — Pete had to remind me. The pyproject.toml version sync was also missed until Pete caught it. Two version files (pyproject.toml + __version__.py) is a maintenance hazard.
do_differently: Always check git status of ALL repos touched before wrapping a session. Consider consolidating version to single source of truth (pyproject.toml dynamic version from __version__.py, or vice versa). Start next session with the Aleph UX plan — it's written and ready.
